_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
condition: Congenital heart disease requiring Atrial Septostomy
clinical_indications: Transposition of the great arteries with or without associated cardiac defects
Pulmonary atresia with intact ventricular septum and total anomalous pulmonary venous connection with restrictive ASD
Hypoplastic left heart syndrome for emergency relief of the trans-septal atrial gradient
Effective treatment in infants less than four to six weeks

preauth.label: Clinical notes with planned line of treatment
Echo report/ Doppler report with stills

claim.label: Post procedure stills of ECHO with report

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes - detailed history, signs &symptoms, indication for procedure
Was the Echo/Doppler report suggestive of Transposition of the Great Arteries or other congenital heart defect necessitating atrial septostomy?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed Procedure / Operative notes submitted?
Does the Post procedure still of ECHO show atrial septal defect?
Is there a Detailed Discharge Summary mentioning date of follow-up submitted?
Is the Invoice of blade/balloon used submitted?

common_queries: Echo/Doppler report does not indicate a congenital heart defect requiring Atrial Septostomy
Clinical notes are incomplete regarding detailed history, signs, symptoms, or indication for the procedure
Post-procedure ECHO stills do not confirm the presence of an atrial septal defect
Detailed Discharge Summary does not mention the date of follow-up
Invoice for the blade/balloon used is missing at claim submission

clinical_key_pointers: Balloon atrial septostomy (BAS) is an interventional procedure undertaken in some cyanotic congenital heart diseases (CHD) with the purpose of widening a restrictive atrial communication.
A balloon is passed through restrictive atrial communication and inflated to a pre-determined size.
Then it is jerkily pulled to IVC-RA junction, thus rupturing and widening the small inter-atrial communication.
A successful procedure allows large inter-atrial communication, significantly improving systemic O2 saturation.
It is an emergency palliative intervention prior to definitive surgery.
